intro: Carex × schmidtiana, also known as Schmidt's sedge, is a perennial, evergreen, and rhizomatous plant species within the Carex genus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of fever, arthritis, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Carex × schmidtiana include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Schmidt's sedge is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical application.
